query: As of July 2026, what is the financial/bankruptcy status of Beyond Meat Inc? Has it filed for Chapter 11 or restructured debt? Any bankruptcy warnings, going concern doubts, debt restructuring, or imminent bankruptcy risk in 2025-2026?
answer:
I've completed comprehensive research on Beyond Meat's financial/bankruptcy status as of July 2026. Here's the summary:

## Key Findings

**No Chapter 11 bankruptcy** — Beyond Meat has NOT filed. It denied August 2025 rumors of an imminent filing and instead executed an out-of-court restructuring.

**Debt restructuring — YES (October 2025):** A $1.15B debt exchange swapped ~97% of the ~$1.1B zero-coupon convertible notes due 2027 for $202.5M of new 7% PIK-toggle notes due 2030 plus ~326M new shares — eliminating ~$900M of debt. Accounted for as a "troubled debt restructuring" (ASC 470-60). This "bought time, not salvation."

**Going concern — NO auditor language:** The FY2025 10-K (filed April 9, 2026) carried an unqualified Deloitte opinion with no going-concern doubt (~$208M cash vs ~$145M annual burn cleared the 12-month bar). However, Deloitte issued an adverse opinion on internal controls due to material weaknesses (inventory/COGS).

**Still severely distressed:**
- Negative shareholder equity ~**-$784M**
- Total debt ~$411.6M; cash ~$205M (Q1 2026)
- $15M minimum liquidity covenant vs $11.8M Q1 cash burn
- **Nasdaq delisting warning** (March 4, 2026); compliance deadline **August 31, 2026**; reverse split likely
- Lost Form S-3 eligibility (equity raises restricted)
- Stock ~**$0.67**, down >99% from IPO peak
- Revenue in structural decline (Q4 2025 -19.7% YoY, volumes down ~22%)

**Verdict:** Bankruptcy was avoided via restructuring, not a court filing. Analysts rate its health "critical"/"survival mode," with residual Chapter 11 risk if the turnaround (rebrand to "Beyond The Plant Protein Company," new beverage lines) fails before liquidity runs out. Full structured findings with 20 data points and 15 sources have been recorded.

sources.snippet: Current price $0.6747; market cap $347.7M; 52-week range $0.50-$7.69; down ~91% from 52-week high.

sources.title: Beyond Meat's Existential Reset: Can Margin Repair Outrun Category Collapse?
sources.snippet: Oct 2025 debt exchange eliminated $900M of 2027 notes, raised $149M via ATM, left $411.6M debt; Q1 2026 cash burn $11.8M vs $15M liquidity covenant; volumes -19.5%; Dec 24, 2025 stock-based debt repayment.

sources.snippet: Debt reduced to $411M, cash $205M; net loss narrowed to $28M; revenues declined 15%; cannot access ATM equity program.

sources.snippet: Restructured $900M convertible notes into 7% PIK-toggle notes (up to 9.5%); 10-K delayed to March 31, 2026 over material weaknesses; delisting risk; bankruptcy risk if new lines fail; 'survival mode.'

sources.title: Beyond Meat faces delisting as it flips the brand
sources.snippet: Nasdaq delisting warning after price below $1 for 30 straight days; deadline Aug 31, 2026; rebranded 'Beyond The Plant Protein Co.'; class action alleging misrepresentation of long-lived assets Feb-Nov 2025.

sources.title: No, Beyond Meat Has Not Filed for Bankruptcy
sources.snippet: Beyond Meat vehemently denied media reports alleging it had filed for bankruptcy amid falling sales.

sources.title: Beyond Meat SEC filing - Loan and Security Agreement notes
sources.snippet: $100M senior secured delayed-draw term loan from Unprocessed Foods (Ahimsa Foundation); $40M drawn June 26 2025, $60M drawn Sept 18 2025; multiple 2025 workforce reductions; China operations suspended.

sources.title: Is Beyond Meat Beyond Repair?
sources.snippet: Had $1.1B debt bill approaching in 2027 it couldn't pay; without restructuring, bankruptcy loomed; stock down over 99% from peak.

sources.title: Beyond Meat Faces Nasdaq Delisting Risk, Eyes Reverse Split
sources.snippet: Nasdaq $1 bid-price warning; company eyeing reverse stock split to regain compliance.

key_facts: Beyond Meat has NOT filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy as of July 2026; it denied August 2025 rumors of an imminent filing.

In October 2025 it completed a $1.15B debt exchange ('troubled debt restructuring'), swapping ~97% of ~$1.1B 0% convertible notes due 2027 for $202.5M of new 7% convertible notes due 2030 (PIK-toggle, up to 9.5%) plus ~326M new shares — eliminating ~$900M of debt.
The restructuring is widely described as having 'saved it from bankruptcy' / 'bought time, not salvation'; it did not fix declining demand.

The FY2025 10-K (filed April 9, 2026) carried an UNQUALIFIED Deloitte opinion with NO going-concern/substantial-doubt language, though Deloitte issued an ADVERSE opinion on internal controls due to material weaknesses (inventory provisions, COGS).
As of Q1 2026: cash ~$205M, total debt ~$411.6M, negative shareholder equity (stockholders' deficit) ~-$784M, total long-term liabilities ~$491.5M.
A $15.0M minimum quarterly liquidity covenant applies (from the 2030 Notes and the fully-drawn $100M Ahimsa/Unprocessed Foods secured term loan); Q1 2026 cash burn was ~$11.8M.
Nasdaq issued a delisting warning on March 4, 2026 (price below $1 for 30 trading days); compliance deadline is August 31, 2026; a reverse stock split is likely.
Company lost Form S-3 eligibility, restricting equity capital raises (ATM access limited).
Q4 2025 net income of +$370.9M was driven entirely by a one-time non-cash TDR gain (~$523–549M); underlying FY2025 operating loss was ~$178M.
Revenue in structural decline: $418M (2022), $343M (2023), $318M (2024); Q1 2026 revenue -15% YoY, Q4 2025 -19.7% YoY, volumes down ~19.5%–22.4%.
Stock ~$0.67 (July 9, 2026), market cap ~$348M, 52-week range $0.50–$7.69, down >99% from IPO-era peak.

Company rebranded to 'Beyond The Plant Protein Company' and is pivoting to functional protein/beverages (Beyond Immerse); analysts rate it 'critical'/'survival mode' with residual Chapter 11 risk if turnaround fails.

data_points.metric: New 2030 convertible notes issued in exchange
Ahimsa/Unprocessed Foods secured term loan facility (fully drawn)
